module.title: Debt Management Techniques
module.description: Learn how to understand, manage, and eliminate debt using proven strategies while building long-term financial stability and healthy money habits.
learning_objectives: Understand how different types of debt work and affect your finances
Learn how credit scores are impacted by debt behavior
Compare debt payoff strategies (snowball vs avalanche)
Build a realistic debt elimination plan
Avoid debt traps and predatory lending practices

lessons.title: Understanding Different Types of Debt
lessons.content: Debt comes in different forms. Secured debt (like car loans or mortgages) is backed by collateral, while unsecured debt (like credit cards) is not. Understanding interest rates and repayment structures helps you avoid long-term financial stress.

lessons.title: How Debt Affects Your Credit Score
lessons.content: Your credit score is influenced by payment history, credit utilization, and length of credit history. High balances and missed payments can significantly lower your score.

lessons.title: Debt-to-Income Ratio (DTI)
lessons.content: DTI measures how much of your income goes toward debt payments. Lenders use it to evaluate your financial health. A lower DTI means better financial stability.

lessons.title: Debt Payoff Strategies That Work
lessons.content: The snowball method focuses on paying smallest debts first for motivation. The avalanche method focuses on highest interest debts first to save money. Choosing the right method depends on your financial and psychological needs.

lessons.title: Debt Consolidation and Refinancing
lessons.content: Debt consolidation combines multiple debts into one payment, often with a lower interest rate. Refinancing replaces an existing loan with a new one under better terms.

lessons.title: Avoiding Future Debt
lessons.content: Building an emergency fund, budgeting properly, and controlling spending habits helps prevent falling back into debt after paying it off.

questions.question: Which factor has the biggest impact on your credit score?
questions.explanation: Payment history is the most important factor in your credit score.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of the avalanche method?
questions.explanation: The avalanche method reduces total interest by targeting high-interest debt first.

questions.question: What does a low debt-to-income ratio indicate?
questions.explanation: A low DTI means you are not overburdened by debt relative to income.
